name: Assertion
image_description: The knight and dragon speak for themselves. This card indicates a quality of reckless heroism, and a refusal to be deterred from the battle.
personal: Sun/Aries people have a strong psychological drive to prove themselves through direct action. Not satisfied by just talking ideas through, they are impatient to get on with doing something about them. These impulsive qualities sometimes indicate the possibility of errors of judgment, but this personality type even finds getting out of a jam a fulfilling challenge. In fact, Sun/Aries people come into their own when faced with a challenging project. Run-of-the-mill activities do not bring out the best talents of people with this planet/sign combination, and finding themselves in this kind of situation, Sun/Aries people do their utmost to bring about change. These personalities have a strong will and much vitality and self-awareness. This can be helpful if one is forced to take the lead, when such qualities can find expression. But the person described by this card usually makes mistakes in acting immediately. Taking extra time and thought helps.
reading: When dealt in a reading, this card shows that the opinions and wishes of others do not easily sway the questioner, and indicates a strong determination to maintain his or her own course in life. The card reveals that the enquirer has a clear vision of what he or she wants, and knows how to achieve it. It also indicates the need to work harder and longer than most to achieve success and the respect of others. The danger when this card appears is one of acting on impulse.
events: Sudden acts, times requiring impulsive courage, an unexpected challenging situation; a need for quick action or defense of one's position.

name: Acquisition
image_description: An excessively exquisitely dressed couple promenade showing their finery for all to see. The symbol above is a ritual voodoo diagram, known as Erzulie, which represents the desire for a marriage bond and shows a love of perfume and jewelry.
personal: Sun/Taurus personalities have a strong sense of purposeful determination and power. They have a natural ability to achieve mastery over physical matter, and, for this reason, often express a sense of the real value of material possessions. The ability of people with this combination to build and make things grow, coupled with their strong need for security, acts as a powerful drive towards achievement. Sun in Taurus characters know exactly what is required to satisfy their every need, and let nothing stand in the way of acquiring this. Emotional and sensitive, people with this aspect have a strong sense of beauty and appreciate the good things in life, traits that make an otherwise practical character a confirmed romantic. Great comfort is highly attractive to the Sun Taurean, and is an incentive in decision-making. But this love of comfort ensures that change doesn't come easily, and an obstinate and willful Taurean nature can resist to the point of detriment.
reading: In a reading, this card shows a strong desire to achieve financial independence, and often indicates the ability to amass a comfortable amount of money. The danger when this card appears is that a natural love for life's pleasures will express itself in greed, over-lavish behavior and ostentation. The card also indicates a need to hold on to possessions, and may indicate a challenge to ownership.
events: Those involving pleasure, artistic encouragement or expensive indulgence; resistance to change; a dispute over possessions; discovery of something of value, a win, gift or a surprise receipt of money.

name: Versatility
image_description: A talented acrobatic figure walks on his hands in order to impress. On the horizon are the symbol of the sun and the alchemical sign for gold. The star above is another ancient symbol for the sun which is, of course, a star.
personal: The Sun/Gemini person's basic drive is communication, and this card indicates a mind that is quick to catch on to things and has little difficulty in expressing new ideas clearly. This card occurs when there is a need to be a "Jack of all trades" and the full gamut of one's talents will be used to the full. It indicates a need for mental and emotional flexibility and versatility in order to secure a positive result. Freedom of action and movement are essential for people with this planet/sign combination, and they suffer real frustration in life as a result of unwelcome ties placed on them by others. The inevitable nerviness this produces means that Sun/Gemini people can waste a lot of energy running in every direction at once. The negative side of this planet/sign combination is the danger of becoming intellectually arrogant, almost forcing ideas and unwanted information on to other people.
reading: When this card appears in a reading, it makes a change of job likely, or perhaps the Gemini characteristic of being able to work on two tasks at once. Words in this situation are important, serving as a strong framework for the question. The Versatility card also indicates a time to open the mind to movement, physical and mental. Contact with brothers, sisters and neighbors is indicated, and many journeys may be required in relation to the question.
events: A social gathering, impressing other people; the need for a quick decision; an important conversation; reliable information; some contact with the media.

name: Resourcefulness
image_description: In a bleak landscape, a raven converts a discarded vase into a highly decorative home.
personal: The Sun/Cancer nature is emotionally sensitive, and considers feelings to be of paramount importance. This card describes a powerfully nurturing person, and whether the question it relates to concerns family, home or business, the same caring qualities come into play. As people with this planet/sign combination experience most things on an emotional level, decisions are based on how they feel rather than cold logic. Sun/Cancer people also have an excellent memory for past life-events. The need for security is prominent for Sun Cancerians, and indeed this card defines a careful person who is happier with a little something put away for a rainy day. Someone with this planet/sign aspect feels a strong need to collect and accumulate both objects and helpful people, and has difficulty in getting rid of things, just in case they come in handy. This shrewd and acquisitive talent is useful in business, in which the Sun Cancerian excels. Often, people with this aspect expend time and energy in making the home a place of beauty and luxury. Such a natural feeling for property and its potential gives a head start in real-estate matters. Sun/Cancer people put down strong roots and it takes a lot to move them once they're settled.
reading: When selected, this card suggests that family background is more than usually important. Inherited family traditions contribute much to the way in which the questioner lives.
events: Those requiring sympathy; suspicion based on previous experience; big family occasions, reunions, inheritance, paternal contact.

name: Fortune
image_description: A benign sun warms the world, its great rays giving a glow of creative life to the small planet.
personal: This is the most auspicious position for the Sun in the whole deck of cards – in its own sign, Leo. Astrologically, Sun in Leo rules the Fifth House, August, prestige, pride, children and love affairs. Sun/Leo people are defined by their dignity and generosity, great vision, and the power to see a plan and make it reality. When this card appears, it has provided, or is about to present, a great, relieved "at last" feeling. Sun in Leo is not about sudden, unexpected good luck, but much more concerned with gaining the recognition or results one has been aiming for. It represents good fortune in the sense that all the hard work put into a project or situation is at last recognized. A good performance is satisfactorily rewarded and one should now take an opportunity to lie back and relax. Although the Fortune card represents the successful end of a long, hard journey, it does not mean that the person it applies to has come to a standstill. The longed-for success has opened a doorway to new horizons and greater responsibility.
reading: There is an element of game-playing when this card appears in a reading. It denotes that whatever has gone before had a long-term strategy, in which each move was countered with a new problem to solve. The enquirer accepted the challenge each time, and surmounted it. When the Fortune card appears with cards that are less auspicious, it has the power to lessen any negatives in a reading.
events: Grand successful plan, great show or achievement, receiving recognition, a creative success, exhibition of talents, successful offspring; love affair.

name: Health
image_description: Three servants tend to the well-being of a princess. A pentacle invokes health and fortune.
personal: The Sun in the sixth sign of the Zodiac indicates a person who has the ability to make order and system out of seeming chaos. This card rules health and work, with the knowledge that material well-being is a requisite for a fulfilling, organized working life. People with this planet/sign combination can be somewhat perfectionist in specialized areas, and, as a result, a little hard on those less able to match their high standards. Care should be taken not to develop too nagging a nature in the name of helping the less organized. A good thinker, the Sun Virgoan values learning in order to analyze and grasp the essence of things. Material progress is also respected by Sun/Virgoans, who have a fondness for the good things in life but do not throw away hard-earned money on rubbish. These abilities flourish in service to others, when the Sun Virgoan is able to give to those in need. Although Sun/Virgo personalities have a great deal of modesty, it is also important that their value is appreciated. Some kind of interest or occupation in diet, nutrition or complementary health is common to the people this card describes. The negative side of the Sun/Virgo combination is a tendency to over-worry about health, which may lead to excessive pill-popping and hypochondria.
reading: When this card is selected, it can indicate a strong need to put one's life in order, and it highlights diet, nutrition and health.
events: Analytical work; a new health regime, concern with physical well-being; cataloging or organizing, correcting or editing work.

name: Harmony
image_description: A beautiful figure sits on a mound and looks up to the heavens. The birds represent spiritual harmony and the transient link between spiritual heaven and material earth.
personal: The basic Sun/Libra nature is easy-going and sociable, with an ability to relate to others in a balanced and co-operative way. Although the House Libra rules is concerned with marriage and close relationships, it has a broader meaning, governing all day-to-day social contacts. Fairness at all costs is important for the Sun Libran: there may be times when the character that this card describes may be seen as indecisive because of the need to weigh up the pros and cons before coming to a conclusion. Plenty of good conversation and amiable discussion is one of his or her greatest pleasures, and a Libran is a good counselor and probably a potentially good diplomat when helping out friends with problems. Sun Librans attract strong, capable and loyal friends, and relationships are of paramount importance to them.
reading: The best successes of these personality types are always shared successes. However, although the Sun Libran undoubtedly works best in partnerships, this card suggests someone who is more of an initiator and who will very often be the member of a team who sets the ball rolling. When one selects the Harmony card, it often indicates a gain from a close relationship, although it can show that one or the other party tends to be pushily dominant. The Harmony card also points to talent in the field of public relations or sales.
events: Situations such as important social gatherings and reunions; extravagant tastes; a difficult decision to make; a pact or agreement, a diplomatic action, being called in to adjudicate a situation.

name: Endurance
image_description: A Herculean figure wrestles to overcome a lion, the symbol of the Leo-ruled Sun.
personal: The Sun/Scorpio combination describes a person aware of the extreme qualities in his or her character. Passion in the broadest sense rules such characters' lives. What they care about, they are devoted to. To some, people with this aspect seem rather secretive, but the truth is that they would rather remain silent than give a watered-down version of what they believe. In order to achieve that which is desired, they are prepared to expend phenomenal energy. Sun Scorpions display a strong sensitivity to the thoughts and feelings of other people, and an intense drive to investigate and understand the nature of things around them. Scorpio is the sign of extremes, and usually indicates a need to take a new path in life. This may manifest as a desire to study such subjects as psychology, philosophy or mysticism. It is obviously good if Sun Scorpions are able to use these abilities in an effective way, and as such people are potential leaders, it would be surprising if they did not run their own business, department or other venture.
reading: When this card is selected, it indicates a compulsive curiosity in the deeper mysteries of life and an interest in self-improvement through strength of will. Often, the card draws attention to a period in which a great challenge must be met with equal tenacity. Some dealings with taxes, legal matters or the handling of the finances of other people will also be prominent.
events: Situations requiring extreme effort or self-control; those involving propaganda or persuasion; power-seeking; an obsessive cause or interest; making a drastic life-change.

name: Enthusiasm
image_description: A bright intelligent head haloed by the sun sits at the top of a traditional alchemical jar. Within the jar, a figure floats among the stars, sun and moon.
personal: Sun/Sagittarius people value honesty, straightforwardness and freedom. They believe in these values not only for themselves, but for others, which makes such characters say what they think and hang the consequences: the Sun Sagittarian may not be much of a diplomat, but is rarely a hypocrite. This card describes someone who is energetic and outgoing, with a good sporty sense of fun and adventure. In fact, if life is a journey, Sun/Sagittarius is one of the best fellow travelers – if friends can keep up. Travel, whether physical or mental, is important for Sun Sagittarians, and the search for the truth may take them long distances in both senses. It is important for people with this planet/sign combination really to know what they believe philosophically. Regardless of educational background, they are able to deal with deeper, more abstract concepts than most people.
reading: When this card is chosen, it indicates an attraction to foreign places and people – to the extent that the person concerned could live for some time in another country. It also reveals a strong intuitive ability that gives sudden flashes of inspiration or visions of future events. The Enthusiasm card in a reading predicts a drive to continue education and to keep up a renewed interest in philosophical, religious or ethical concepts. The downside of this card is the chance that one is about to let out a home truth that might hurt someone's feelings.
events: Splendid opportunities; contact from abroad, the prospect of long-distance travel; a new philosophy, optimism about a new project.

name: Achievement
image_description: A fortified city over which a large impressive palace presides. In the sky is an opulent sun, and, above it, an idol in the form of a goat.
personal: A Sun/Capricorn combination is a strong indication of someone who is a conscientious and ambitious organizer. This mix produces excellent intuitive faculties that can be put to good use in achieving life aims. The goal for people with this planet/sign combination is a slow and steady advancement, based on solid ground and security-oriented. This card describes those who find it easy to reach a responsible position in a community, and it is here that their abilities lie. Sun/Capricorn people have a good talent for organizing and thrive in an executive post at whatever level feels comfortable to them. He or she will have the patience to work hard in obtaining the necessary skills to achieve honor and recognition, and the Sun in this sign shows an undoubted strong ambition to attain power and authority. If the person this card describes has all these typical qualities, he or she has a lot to look forward to: the phrase "life begins at forty" will have many pleasurable connotations. Negative aspects to this combination of qualities could push the Sun Capricornian towards an unscrupulous desire to get on in life no matter the cost. There can also be a tendency to exploit others.
reading: Often when one receives this card in a reading it indicates a powerful moral sense and a feeling of obligation to set a good example for others. This may be because one has been chosen or promoted to a role.
events: Commitment to a project, great responsibility; recommendation or distinction in some activity; help of influential or famous people.

name: Idealism
image_description: A minstrel pipes accompaniment to the eccentric journey of a dignitary casually riding a mule. The rider carries a cornucopia in one hand and a wand in the other. Above, an angel pours the free-flowing Aquarian wisdom on those below.
personal: The Sun/Aquarius character could be defined as having an unusual, determined and sometimes stubborn temperament. Such types are not too concerned about what others think about their beliefs and behavior, and so may be considered "original" if not downright eccentric. Questioning old, "stick-in-the-mud" ways and exploring the new is very much what Aquarians are about. Mental achievements are most important to Sun/Aquarius types, and their quest is a constant exchange of ideas with others in order to fix their own beliefs. Their needs cause them to gather around them friends and acquaintances of all shapes, sizes, creeds and colors. Sun Aquarians value interesting friendships with inventive or scientific people, and anything new, novel, scientific or gadgety also has appeal. Essentially a person of the twenty-first century, the Sun Aquarian keeps up with the times and supports the causes that take us into the future. It is with this latter concern that people with this planet/sign combination may find real satisfaction, bringing their sympathy for human problems to idealistic and good causes.
reading: When this card occurs in a reading, it often indicates help from friends with power and influence. It shows that taking the lead in a group activity could be rewarding, especially if the cause arouses the questioner's humanitarian instincts.
events: Eccentric behavior, an original idea or invention, involvement in an attention-seeking act; sudden upset; lateral thinking; an influential acquaintance gives a helping hand.

name: Vision
image_description: At the edge of the sea is an image of the sun half below the water. It is unclear whether it is rising or setting. A lynx follows the gaze of the sun. Above, the single eye represents the inner eye of psychic vision.
personal: The Sun/Pisces card describes the kind of person who is extremely sensitive to the thoughts and feelings of others. In many ways, this sympathy and feeling for others can easily seduce the Sun Piscean into becoming and doing what others want. The opposite side of the coin, however, is the pop star, fashion leader or ad-man who knows exactly how to tap into and exploit the mass feelings of the moment. Most Pisceans have a little of each quality in their make-up. One thing is certain – they are among the most imaginative people in the zodiac, and, given a suitable outlet for this talent for day-dreaming, they are made in life. With the Sun in this sign, the Piscean will is directed to exploring his or her own unconscious and imaginative resources. It seems that no matter how successful Piscean people become at making their own way in life, they have a strong need to give, look after, and make sacrifices for others.
reading: When this card is selected, the questioner may be dreaming of escape in the form of a long-distance voyage or other "get-away-from-it-all" activity. The Vision card may also indicate a new interest, and very often some ability in psychology and/or psychic research. It suggests that at some time in the future, the person asking the question may be involved with a large institution, and that service to others will provide great satisfaction, recognition and fulfillment.
events: A sacrifice; a mystery or a confusion of imagination and reality; a chance to escape; a miracle; situations in which one loses the plot.
